title: OpenAI's Plan To Merge Chat And Agents — With Greg Brockman
summary: Greg Brockman, OpenAI president and co-founder, discusses OpenAI's vision to merge chat and agents into persistent personal AGI systems. He argues that compute will ultimately decide the AI race, that interfaces will eventually dissolve into autonomous agents acting on users' behalf, and that current premium intelligence will become tomorrow's commodity as pricing pressures intensify.
writeup: Greg Brockman, OpenAI president and co-founder, lays out a plan to fold chat and agents into persistent personal AGI systems that act on a user's behalf. The conversation frames compute as the deciding factor in the AI race and predicts that interfaces will dissolve into autonomous agents over time. Brockman also argues that today's premium intelligence will become a commodity as pricing pressure builds. The discussion ties these shifts to scaling, large language models, and test-time compute.
